issue.title: Block Urgently phishing wallet sites
issue.body: ### Prerequisites - [x] I checked the documentation and found no answer; - [x] I checked to make sure that this issue has not already been filed; - [x] This is not an ad/bug report. ### Problem description Fake websites like Exodus Wallet, Safepal Wallet, and Tronlink Wallet need to be blocked immediately. They steal users' seed phrases daily and use cloaking. They were previously banned, and for unknown reasons, it was lifted. How is this possible? Please do not protect these scammers. You can access scam content through Bing, as they are cloaking. https://safepal.com.co/ https://tronlink.com.co/ https://exodus.com.ag/ Virustotal report: https://www.virustotal.com/gui/url/694e015657f63f4aa883e6186031b8e4acfa950a756e446a4dc2b6627617e7be (exodus.com.ag) https://www.virustotal.com/gui/domain/tronlink.com.co (tronlink.com.co) https://www.virustotal.com/gui/domain/safepal.com.co (safepal.com.co) ### Proposed solution There are a huge number of detections everywhere, but the domain registrar doesn't block it and covers it up, since most likely they are in cahoots with it! ### Additional information

comment.body: Unfortunately, the domains are still active, and the scammer continues to steal hundreds of thousands of dollars daily. What's the point of this group then? No one even responded to me.

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.title: alfalfalfa.com

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

New change of tinyShield's build script for Apple Safari Extension user is available.

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

labels.description: Issues, which cannot be resolved easily

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://private-adobe-connect-meeting.link/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fprivate-adobe-connect-meeting.link%2F) ### Comment > I am unable to join Adobe Connect meetings via the Web client (HTML view) on Windows using Google Chrome. The browser throws an error asking to check the troubleshooting page. After debugging, I found that the issue is caused by the Persian Blocker filter in AdGuard, which is blocking and modifying essential scripts required for the Adobe Connect web interface to launch. > > When AdGuard is disabled (or when using Incognito mode without the extension), the web client loads perfectly. > > > (Note: The actual URLs and session IDs in the logs below have been replaced with private-adobe-connect-meating.link for privacy reasons, but the script paths and structural data remain exactly as they appeared in the original logs.) > > Filtering Logs: > > Log 1 (Blocked JavaScript): > The filter is blocking the meetingLaunchIA.js script, which is critical for Adobe Connect to initialize. > > Status: > HTTP status code: ---- > ---- > HTTP request method: GET > > Filtering status: > Blocked > > URL: > https://private-adobe-connect-meating.link/common/scripts/analytics/meetingLaunchIA.js?ver=10.8.0 > > Type: > JavaScript > > Source: > private-adobe-connect-meating.link > > Assumed rule: > *||ac.ir/common/scripts/analytics/meetingLaunchIA.js^$script,~third-party > > Filter: > Persian Blocker > Log 2 (Modified HTML): > The filter is also applying a scriptlet modification that seems to interfere with the session environment. > > Filtering status: > Modified > > URL: > https://private-adobe-connect-meating.link/SUBDOMAIN_path_data?session=breezbreezz24ocxxkrv722iih&html-view=true > > Type: > HTML > > Source: > private-adobe-connect-meating.link > > Applied rule: > ac.ir#%#//scriptlet('ubo-set', 'chromeOS', 'true') > > Filter: > Persian Blocker ``` Username: @ali291384 ``` ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/rte4y7myuggokwkgckkkwsocscsc0ocf7um6u3hyr4.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/4zeh5vex51c0g8wo8ck040840o8ok0wra2to19sd1c.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard Browser Extension v5.3.1.63 MV3 System version: | `Windows 11` Browser: | Chrome Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

**Other:**
Filter unblocking search ads and self-promotion

**Language-specific:**
Persian Blocker Filters last update: | 2026-04-29T09:01:17 Tracking protection: | disabled ### Issue configuration [Import](https://reports.adguard.com/settings/import.html?product_type=ext&product_version=5.3.1.63&system_version=Windows+11&manifest_version=3&browser=chrome&filters=2.10.235&browsing_security.statistics_enabled=false&stealth.enabled=false&filters_last_update=2026-04-29T09%3A01%3A17)

issue.body:
# Sorry. The website is infected by AdShield, Inc.

Unfortunately, It is complicated to resolve with a regular adblock rule because the website is being protected by service of Ad-Shield that is an anti-adblock company.

## Userscript Installation
Please block Ad-Shield advertisement by installing [tinyShield userscript](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield).
Please check its installation guideline written in English by visiting [this URL](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.md).

If you can understand Korean language, please refer https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/blob/main/README.ko.md.

New change of tinyShield's build script for Apple Safari Extension user (made at 2026 Apr 21) is available.

## Still Have a Problem?
An issue is occurred when the userscript is applied, please report to [this issue tracker](https://github.com/FilteringDev/tinyShield/issues).

issue.title: private-adobe-connect-meeting.link

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

labels.name: A: In progress
labels.description: Work on the issue is in progress

labels.name: Stale
labels.description: The issue was not processed on time and was bumped to the top of the list

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://private-adobe-connect-meeting.link/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fprivate-adobe-connect-meeting.link%2F) ### Comment > I am unable to join Adobe Connect meetings via the Web client (HTML view) on Windows using Google Chrome. The browser throws an error asking to check the troubleshooting page. After debugging, I found that the issue is caused by the Persian Blocker filter in AdGuard, which is blocking and modifying essential scripts required for the Adobe Connect web interface to launch. > > When AdGuard is disabled (or when using Incognito mode without the extension), the web client loads perfectly. > > > (Note: The actual URLs and session IDs in the logs below have been replaced with private-adobe-connect-meating.link for privacy reasons, but the script paths and structural data remain exactly as they appeared in the original logs.) > > Filtering Logs: > > Log 1 (Blocked JavaScript): > The filter is blocking the meetingLaunchIA.js script, which is critical for Adobe Connect to initialize. > > Status: > HTTP status code: ---- > ---- > HTTP request method: GET > > Filtering status: > Blocked > > URL: > https://private-adobe-connect-meating.link/common/scripts/analytics/meetingLaunchIA.js?ver=10.8.0 > > Type: > JavaScript > > Source: > private-adobe-connect-meating.link > > Assumed rule: > *||ac.ir/common/scripts/analytics/meetingLaunchIA.js^$script,~third-party > > Filter: > Persian Blocker > Log 2 (Modified HTML): > The filter is also applying a scriptlet modification that seems to interfere with the session environment. > > Filtering status: > Modified > > URL: > https://private-adobe-connect-meating.link/SUBDOMAIN_path_data?session=breezbreezz24ocxxkrv722iih&html-view=true > > Type: > HTML > > Source: > private-adobe-connect-meating.link > > Applied rule: > ac.ir#%#//scriptlet('ubo-set', 'chromeOS', 'true') > > Filter: > Persian Blocker ``` Username: @ali291384 ``` ### Screenshots
Screenshot 1 ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/rte4y7myuggokwkgckkkwsocscsc0ocf7um6u3hyr4.png?nc=1)
Screenshot 1 with AdGuard disabled ![Screenshot 1](https://cdn.adguardcdn.com/sitereports/4zeh5vex51c0g8wo8ck040840o8ok0wra2to19sd1c.png?nc=1)
### System configuration | Information | Value | |-------------|-------| AdGuard product: | AdGuard Browser Extension v5.3.1.63 MV3 System version: | `Windows 11` Browser: | Chrome Filters: | **Ad Blocking:**
AdGuard Base filter

**Other:**
Filter unblocking search ads and self-promotion

**Language-specific:**
Persian Blocker Filters last update: | 2026-04-29T09:01:17 Tracking protection: | disabled ### Issue configuration [Import](https://reports.adguard.com/settings/import.html?product_type=ext&product_version=5.3.1.63&system_version=Windows+11&manifest_version=3&browser=chrome&filters=2.10.235&browsing_security.statistics_enabled=false&stealth.enabled=false&filters_last_update=2026-04-29T09%3A01%3A17)

Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).
